segments.text: He answered me, {
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The Most High has made this world for many, but the world to come for few.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now I will tell you a parable, Esdras. Just as when you ask the earth, it will say to you that it gives very much clay from which earthen vessels are made, but little dust that gold comes from. Even so is the course of the present world.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Many have been created, but few will be saved.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I answered, {
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Drink your fill of understanding then, O my soul, and let my heart devour wisdom.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For you have come here apart from your will, and depart against your will, for you have only been given a short time to live.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: O Lord over us, grant to your servant that we may pray before you, and give us seed for our heart and cultivation for our understanding, that fruit may grow from it, by which everyone who is corrupt, who bears the likeness of a man, may live.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For you alone exist, and we all one workmanship of your hands, just as you have said.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Because you give life to the body that is now fashioned in the womb, and give it members, your creature is preserved in fire and water, and your workmanship endures nine months as your creation which is created in it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But that which keeps and that which is kept will both be kept by your keeping. When the womb gives up again what has grown in it,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: you have commanded that out of the parts of the body, that is to say, out of the breasts, be given milk, which is the fruit of the breasts,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: that the body that is fashioned may be nourished for a time, and afterwards you guide it in your mercy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Yes, you have brought it up in your righteousness, nurtured it in your law, and corrected it with your judgment.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You put it to death as your creation, and make it live as your work.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: If therefore you lightly and suddenly destroy him which with so great labor was fashioned by your commandment, to what purpose was he made?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now therefore I will speak. About man in general, you know best, but about your people for whose sake I am sorry,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and for your inheritance, for whose cause I mourn, for Israel, for whom I am heavy, and for the seed of Jacob, for whose sake I am troubled,
segments.speaker: Phaltiel

segments.text: therefore I will begin to pray before you for myself and for them; for I see the failings of us who dwell in the land;
segments.speaker: Phaltiel

segments.text: but I have heard the swiftness of the judgment which is to come.
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: Therefore hear my voice, and understand my saying, and I will speak before you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The beginning of the words of Esdras, before he was taken up. He said,
O Lord, you who remain forever, whose eyes are exalted, and whose chambers are in the air,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: whose throne is beyond measure, whose glory is beyond comprehension, before whom the army of angels st and with trembling,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: at whose bidding they are changed to wind and fire, whose word is sure, and sayings constant, whose ordinance is strong, and commandment fearful,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: whose look dries up the depths, and whose indignation makes the mountains to melt away, and whose truth bears witness—
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: hear, O Lord, the prayer of your servant, and give ear to the petition of your handiwork.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Attend to my words, for as long as I live, I will speak, and as long as I have understanding, I will answer.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don’t look at the sins of your people, but on those who have served you in truth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don’t regard the doings of those who act wickedly, but of those who have kept your covenants in affliction.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don’t think about those who have lived wickedly before you, but remember those who have willingly known your fear.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Let it not be your will to destroy those who have lived like cattle, but look at those who have clearly taught your law.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don’t be indignant at those who are deemed worse than animals, but love those who have always put their trust in your glory.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For we and our fathers have passed our lives in ways that bring death, but you are called merciful because of us sinners.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: For if you have a desire to have mercy upon us who have no works of righteousness, then you will be called merciful.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For the just, which have many good works laid up with you, will be rewarded for their own deeds.
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: For what is man, that you should take displeasure at him? Or what is a corruptible race, that you should be so bitter toward it?
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: For in truth, there is no man among those who are born who has not done wickedly, and among those who have lived, there is none which have not done wrong.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: For in this, O Lord, your righteousness and your goodness will be declared, if you are merciful to those who have no store of good works.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Then he answered me, {
Some things you have spoken rightly, and it will happen according to your words.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For indeed I will not think about the fashioning of those who have sinned, or about their death, their judgment, or their destruction;
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: but I will rejoice over the creation of the righteous and their pilgrimage, their salvation, and the reward that they will have.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Therefore as I have spoken, so it will be.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For as the farmer sows many seeds in the ground, and plants many trees, and yet not all that is sown will come up in due season, neither will all that is planted take root, even so those who are sown in the world will not all be saved.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then I answered, {
If I have found favor, let me speak before you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If the farmer’s seed doesn’t come up because it hasn’t received your rain in due season, or if it is ruined by too much rain and perishes,
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: likewise man, who is formed with your hands and is called your own image, because he is made like you, for whose sake you have formed all things, even him you have made like the farmer’s seed.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Don’t be angry with us, but spare your people and have mercy upon your inheritance, for you have mercy upon your own creation.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Then he answered me, {
Things present are for those who live now, and things to come for those who will live hereafter.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For you come far short of being able to love my creature more than I. But you have compared yourself to the unrighteous. Don’t do that!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yet in this will you be admirable to the Most High,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: in that you have humbled yourself, as it becomes you, and have not judged yourself among the righteous, so as to be much glorified.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For many grievous miseries will fall on those who dwell in the world in the last times, because they have walked in great pride.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But understand for yourself, and for those who inquire concerning the glory of those like you,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: because paradise is opened to you. The tree of life is planted. The time to come is prepared. Plenteousness is made ready. A city is built. Rest is allowed. Goodness is perfected, and wisdom is perfected beforehand.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The root of evil is sealed up from you. Weakness is done away from you, and death is hidden. Hell and corruption have fled into forgetfulness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Sorrows have passed away, and in the end, the treasure of immortality is shown.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore ask no more questions concerning the multitude of those who perish.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For when they had received liberty, they despised the Most High, scorned his law, and forsook his ways.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Moreover they have trodden down his righteous,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and said in their heart that there is no God—even knowing that they must die.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For as the things I have said will welcome you, so thirst and pain which are prepared for them. For the Most High didn’t intend that men should be destroyed,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: but those who are created have themselves defiled the name of him who made them, and were unthankful to him who prepared life for them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore my judgment is now at hand,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: which I have not shown to all men, but to you, and a few like you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then I answered,
Behold, O Lord, now you have shown me the multitude of the wonders which you will do in the last times, but you haven’t shown me when.
segments.speaker: Esdras
